reasoning: A market-wide circuit breaker (Level 1, 7% drop) in the S&P 500 is a very rare event, last occurring in March 2020. During early April 2025, the market experienced extreme volatility due to tariff fears, with the S&P 500 dropping 6% on April 4, but missing the 7% threshold required to trigger a circuit breaker. Multiple authoritative sources confirm that no circuit breaker was triggered during this sell-off, and there is no news of any circuit breaker triggering through April 27. With only 3 days left until the April 30 deadline, the probability of a 7% one-day drop occurring is extremely low. The market price on April 17 was already at 5%, and the passage of time without an incident further pushes the probability to the absolute minimum.
